title: Black Light Attack!

jokes.text: hey, kenneth, why aren't your teeth glowing in the black light? you'll have to ask the fella who whittled 'em for me.
jokes.setup: Question about why Kenneth's teeth don't glow under black light
jokes.punchline: Reveals his teeth were hand-carved by someone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdism - only he would have whittled teeth. Excellent character integration and originality.

jokes.text: A month ago i was doing a robot act on the street, so i know my place. i want to be tgs' steve nash. come down from canada, work hard, make the black guy look good.
jokes.setup: Danny establishing his gratitude and place on the show
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Steve Nash with the specific goal of making Tracy look good
jokes.explanation: Solid sports reference with edge. Good structure but relies on sports knowledge for full impact.

jokes.text: look at me in the black light.it's tron. watch out, sark.It's me, flynn. light bike.
jokes.setup: Danny is glowing from robot body paint under black light
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Tron reference with character names
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that's a bit overextended. Visual element helps but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Robot lorenzo lamas was funny. as was robot ryan seacrest and david hasselbot.
jokes.setup: Praising Danny's robot impressions
jokes.punchline: David Hasselbot - portmanteau of Hasselhoff and robot
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with 'Hasselbot' - good economy and quotable.

jokes.text: it's not what you think! it's something i need to wear to support my breasts!
jokes.setup: Jack assumes Danny's bra is sexual
jokes.punchline: Liz's overly clinical explanation about breast support
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz awkwardness and TMI moment. Good character work.

jokes.text: lemon, i have season tickets to every sports team in new york. so close to the action, you'll feellike you're sitting in front of an hd television
jokes.setup: Jack boasting about premium sports tickets
jokes.punchline: Comparing the live experience to watching TV at home
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - premium seats compared to the home viewing experience everyone actually prefers.

jokes.text: but my biggest problem with quiddich is, if the snitch is 150 points, why does anyone bother with the quaffle?
jokes.setup: Liz discussing Harry Potter at a sports event
jokes.punchline: Applying logical analysis to fictional sport rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz nerdiness - overthinking fictional sports rules. Great character integration and became a cultural reference.

jokes.text: hey, jeter! Are you jealous?i'm with my new boyfriend!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to show off at baseball game
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly yelling at famous baseball player about her relationship
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Liz's social awkwardness and desperation.

jokes.text: that girl has a name, jack.we call her 'skankovitch.'
jokes.setup: Jack referring to Russian dancer dismissively
jokes.punchline: Liz defending her dignity then revealing equally crude nickname
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Liz appears to defend the woman then reveals equally offensive nickname. Great structure.

jokes.text: oh, mr.Donaghy.i forgot to give you the factory worker death rates.
jokes.setup: Jack describing office seduction scenarios
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of worker death rates as flirtation excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Jack humor - casualizing corporate death statistics as workplace flirtation. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: and you take your reward. you take your reward.
jokes.setup: Jack describing office hookups
jokes.punchline: Repeated creepy euphemism getting increasingly uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation with repetition making it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: how drunk are you? a lot to very.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Jack's intoxication level
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact quantification using qualitative terms
jokes.explanation: Great economy and deadpan delivery. 'A lot to very' became quotable.

jokes.text: is it the body paint or is danny just glowinglike a beacon of manly camaraderie?
jokes.setup: Jack drunk and confused about Danny's appeal
jokes.punchline: Overwrought poetic description of male friendship
jokes.explanation: Good Jack drunk logic with poetic language for simple concepts.

jokes.text: liz, i can't do girls'lunch today. we've never done that.
jokes.setup: Jenna canceling plans with Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz pointing out they've never actually done this
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with economical correction of Jenna's assumption.

jokes.text: i play tartine gramercy, an heiress to a vermouth fortune and a freshman at nyu.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her audition role
jokes.punchline: Absurd character name and oddly specific vermouth fortune
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist character creation typical of fake TV shows in 30 Rock universe.

jokes.text: it's interesting that they highlighted the mother's lines. well, that's so i'll know what part not to read.
jokes.setup: Liz noticing the mother's lines are highlighted
jokes.punchline: Jenna's oblivious explanation missing the obvious implication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna obliviousness and vanity - can't conceive she'd be cast as the mother.

jokes.text: oh, pete, that's later.maybe we'll be dead by then.
jokes.setup: Pete worried about Jenna's reaction to learning she's the mother
jokes.punchline: Liz's casual hope for death to avoid the situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Liz humor - preferring death to dealing with Jenna's drama. Great economy.

jokes.text: what?It's true.she does look like one of those.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions Tracy's remarks about Madeleine Albright
jokes.punchline: Tracy doubling down on his inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but relies on cheap shock value rather than clever construction.

jokes.text: even you foreigners.
jokes.setup: Tracy learning to respect women
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating he hasn't learned by being casually xenophobic
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure showing Tracy's continued obliviousness despite stated goal.

jokes.text: 10:00 a.m.dotcom shows up pictures From his trip to greece. i think i'm gonna go ahead and cancel that.
jokes.setup: Reading the day's schedule
jokes.punchline: Immediately canceling Dotcom's vacation slide show
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's disinterest in others' experiences.

jokes.text: i like to go to tasti dlite. pinkberry?
jokes.setup: Tracy refusing strip clubs, asking Sue to choose activity
jokes.punchline: Sue's innocent suggestion of frozen yogurt shops
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between entourage expectations and innocent reality.

jokes.text: oh, come on, you hoser!hoser!
jokes.setup: Sue getting frustrated with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Canadian woman's idea of aggressive language is 'hoser'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific comedy playing on Canadian stereotypes and innocent aggression.

jokes.text: hey, is it that chick lawyer who does the sexual harassment presentation? because she's asking for it.
jokes.setup: Jack guessing about Danny's hookup
jokes.punchline: Ironic comment about sexual harassment lawyer
jokes.explanation: Dark irony appropriate for Jack but relies somewhat on shock value.

jokes.text: even though the reason is she's never let a man see her feet.
jokes.setup: Danny describing woman who keeps shoes on during intimacy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre foot-related hang-up
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail that feels like genuine character quirk.

jokes.text: mostly we just kiss and then she makes mego down to the deli to get her a sandwich.
jokes.setup: Describing physical relationship
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic revelation about sandwich errands
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from romantic to mundane. Well-structured deflation.

jokes.text: they're called spanx.
jokes.setup: Danny describing mysterious underwear
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just shapewear
jokes.explanation: Good economy and misdirection. Audience realizes he's describing Liz.

jokes.text: what do you mean?I'm not playing the mo
jokes.setup: Jenna realizing she's reading the wrong character
jokes.punchline: Cut off mid-word as reality hits
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with perfect timing of realization.

jokes.text: black light attack!
jokes.setup: Jack trying to change subject about Liz's personal life
jokes.punchline: Random callback to episode title/black light theme
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that doesn't add much beyond episode title reference.

jokes.text: hey, liz.i just want to show you this new iphone app. you shake it and it soundslike an empty can.
jokes.setup: Danny coming to Liz's office late
jokes.punchline: Pointless app that recreates mundane sound
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist app idea that captures pointless tech innovation.

jokes.text: my favorite apps are the ones before my entree.
jokes.setup: Danny talking about iPhone apps
jokes.punchline: Liz misunderstands, thinks about appetizers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz food-obsession and wordplay. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: what did he do to the back of your knees?
jokes.setup: Jack noticing something about Liz
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific intimate detail
jokes.explanation: Good cringe specificity. Back of knees is weird enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: hey, watch your mouth!She's only 34 years old!
jokes.setup: Someone asking Tracy if he likes Italian ice
jokes.punchline: Tracy thinks they're insulting Sue with 'Italian ice'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and protective Tracy showing character growth.

jokes.text: when they were little, i threw them in the deep end of our pool to help them get over their fearof sharks.
jokes.setup: Tracy comparing treatment of sons vs daughters
jokes.punchline: Illogical parenting method mixing pool and ocean fears
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy logic - completely backwards but delivered with confidence. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: the mother. they wanted me to play the...mother.
jokes.setup: Jenna returning from audition
jokes.punchline: Devastated realization delivered with horror
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup. Jenna's vanity makes this devastating.

jokes.text: would a mother be planning a sex tour of vietnam this spring?
jokes.setup: Jenna denying she could be a mother
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate vacation plan as evidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Jenna humor. 'Sex tour of Vietnam' is memorably specific and wrong.

jokes.text: but those were my majors at the royal tampa academy of dramatic tricks.
jokes.setup: Liz saying Jenna can't play young roles forever
jokes.punchline: Jenna's ridiculous acting school and specialties
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist institution name and bizarre curriculum. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: you can be like Madonna and cling to youth with your gollum arms
jokes.setup: Liz giving Jenna advice about aging
jokes.punchline: Brutal description of Madonna's arms
jokes.explanation: 'Gollum arms' is memorably brutal. Good visual and pop culture reference.

jokes.text: Mmm, no.That won't be necessary...officer.
jokes.setup: Danny offering to change clothes
jokes.punchline: Liz's awkward sexual comment about police costume
jokes.explanation: Good Liz awkward sexual moment. The 'officer' adds perfect cringe element.

jokes.text: this is so tandem. 'random,' jenna.Those kids are saying the word'random.'
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to use youth slang
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'random' as 'tandem'
jokes.explanation: Good aging character trying too hard to be young. Simple but effective malapropism.

jokes.text: hang on.i'm just tweeting that i ran into you. this is so tandem.
jokes.setup: Jenna acting young and social media savvy
jokes.punchline: Using 'tandem' instead of 'random'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's attempt to be young and current falling flat.

jokes.text: they use it in japan to keep kids from loitering outside of convenience stores.
jokes.setup: Explaining high-pitched tone device
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific real-world application
jokes.explanation: Good weird real-world detail. Frank's nerd knowledge is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: oh!My ears are, like, dying.Facebook!
jokes.setup: Cerie reacting to high-pitched tone
jokes.punchline: Randomly exclaiming 'Facebook' while in pain
jokes.explanation: Good young character moment but Facebook reference feels forced.

jokes.text: i also have this lowfrequency tonethat can only be heard by people over 40. have you started playing it yet?
jokes.setup: Frank mentioning another age-specific tone
jokes.punchline: Jack's paranoid question about whether it's already playing
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of the age test concept. Jack's paranoia is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: this is from danny's chips costume.what is it doing here?
jokes.setup: Jack finding evidence of Danny being in Liz's office
jokes.punchline: Chips costume piece as evidence of their hookup
jokes.explanation: Standard detective work setup. Visual gag works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: this badge is a symbol of dignity, honor, and dramatized 1970s interethnic california daytime motorcycle highway justice.
jokes.setup: Danny talking about his police costume badge
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and ridiculous description of justice
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating specificity. 'Dramatized 1970s interethnic california daytime motorcycle highway justice' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: with their untucked shirts, boneless faces, their stars, both wars and trek.
jokes.setup: Jack complaining about the men at work
jokes.punchline: List culminating in Star Wars/Star Trek reference
jokes.explanation: Great Jack disdain. 'Boneless faces' and 'stars, both wars and trek' are perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: little scrump nugget.
jokes.setup: Jack describing Liz's relationship with Danny
jokes.punchline: Diminutive pet name 'scrump nugget'
jokes.explanation: Great made-up pet name. 'Scrump nugget' is memorably specific and condescending.

jokes.text: i'll never be like you!
jokes.setup: Sue arguing with Tracy about staying in
jokes.punchline: Teenage rebellion cliché from grown woman
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of parent-child dynamic with Tracy becoming protective father figure.

jokes.text: Can't make me stay here! you are a part of this entourage. i didn't ask to bein this entourage! look!I know you didn't mean that. i do mean it.I'm not like you! i'll never be like you!
jokes.setup: Sue rebelling against Tracy's entourage
jokes.punchline: The teenage daughter-like rebellion complete with dramatic declarations
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the father-daughter dynamic, playing out like a classic teen rebellion scene.

jokes.text: she has really thin lips, but she makes upFor it with tongue girth.
jokes.setup: Danny describing kissing with his mystery woman
jokes.punchline: Graphic description using 'tongue girth'
jokes.explanation: 'Tongue girth' is memorably gross. Good cringe factor but relies on shock.

jokes.text: Our latest information on women's sexuality finds female libido does tend to peak in those years just beforeperimenopause In what scientists and doctors like myselfare calling their dirty 30s.
jokes.setup: Medical discussion about female sexuality
jokes.punchline: The clinical term 'dirty 30s'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Liz's later line. The mock-scientific 'dirty 30s' term is memorable.

jokes.text: i guess i'm gettinga second wind here at the tail endof my dirty 30s.
jokes.setup: Jack calling her behavior a low blow
jokes.punchline: Liz referencing news segment about women's peak sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good callback to news segment. Shows Liz applying information to herself.

jokes.text: i wrote it last night...late. the shirt should be... Filthier.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining when she wrote the sketch
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual costume direction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Liz's sexual awakening affecting her work.

jokes.text: if you were a man, you would have to register yourself as a sex criminal.
jokes.setup: Jack observing Liz's inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Extreme comparison to sex offenders
jokes.explanation: Strong dark humor about double standards. Good escalation and Jack-appropriate moral panic.

jokes.text: pretending i wasn't fourth runnerup at the miss teen bicentennial pageant.
jokes.setup: Jenna listing things she does to maintain her image
jokes.punchline: Specific pageant title that dates her
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail that reveals Jenna's age while being character-appropriate. 'Fourth runner-up' is perfect specificity.

jokes.text: if you come out as your real age,i will reveal my friend tom.
jokes.setup: Liz offering to reveal a secret in exchange
jokes.punchline: Mysterious 'friend Tom' as her secret
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Tom Selleck reveal. Creates intrigue about Liz's secret.

jokes.text: tom selleck.he's my moustache.
jokes.setup: Liz revealing her hidden friend Tom
jokes.punchline: Tom Selleck mustache reveal
jokes.explanation: Exceptional visual gag and character reveal. Tom Selleck mustache is perfectly absurd and memorable. Great setup/payoff.

jokes.text: All my 'aha' moments end with a moustache pressed against me.
jokes.setup: Jenna responding to Liz's mustache reveal
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific pattern in Jenna's life revelations
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific response. Implies strange romantic history with mustachioed men.

jokes.text: not for a billion doll hairs. i'm sorry.Did you say 'doll hairs'? yeah, they're not worth nothing.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he wouldn't trade the experience
jokes.punchline: Malapropism 'doll hairs' instead of 'dollars' and defending it
jokes.explanation: Great malapropism that Tracy doubles down on. 'Doll hairs' became a cultural reference.

jokes.text: i'm entranced by those mudcolored eyes, Set back in... that skin.
jokes.setup: Jack pretending to be in love with Liz
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliments about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack attempting romance but being insulting. 'Mud-colored eyes' and 'that skin' are memorably unflattering.

jokes.text: that splayfooted walk.
jokes.setup: Jack continuing his 'romantic' description
jokes.punchline: Clinical description of Liz's gait
jokes.explanation: 'Splay-footed' is perfectly clinical and unromantic. Great escalation of backhanded compliments.

jokes.text: the nagano olympics. microsoft windows '95. but i'm 41 now.time to die.
jokes.setup: Jenna as dying character listing life experiences
jokes.punchline: Recent cultural events as if they were ancient history
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist aging where recent events are treated as ancient. Shows Jenna's new perspective on age.

jokes.text: It started out as a joke, but it's becoming real.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his fake love confession
jokes.punchline: Admitting his lie is becoming real feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's unexpected emotional development. Solid but not exceptional.

tmdb_overview: Jack tries to squash the burgeoning relationship between Liz and Danny. Tracy asks Sue to be in his entourage. Jenna tries out for Gossip Girl.
insight: Season 4's densest episode: 63 jokes in 22 minutes, led by character chaos and escalation.
